fingerprint: Aetheric redshift scale λ_A = c/H₀ = 4,283 Mpc is identically the Hubble radius — a standard cosmological constant derived from expanding-universe observations. Redshift formula z = D/λ_A is algebraically identical to Hubble's Law z = H₀d/c.
globe_value: Hubble radius d_H = c/H₀ ≈ 4,283 Mpc (using H₀ ≈ 70 km/s/Mpc). This is a fundamental parameter of expanding-universe cosmology: the distance at which the recession velocity equals c.
dome_value: λ_A = 4,283 Mpc, called 'aetheric characteristic wavelength' or 'aetheric redshift scale.' Defined as c/H₀ on model.html.
match_precision: Exact by construction — defined using the same formula (c/H₀) with the same H₀ value
why_it_matters: The dome's entire redshift mechanism is algebraically identical to Hubble's Law. The formula z = D/λ_A where λ_A = c/H₀ is literally z = H₀D/c, which IS Hubble's Law for low redshift. The dome has changed the interpretation (from cosmic expansion to aetheric attenuation) but not the mathematics. This is not a derivation from dome geometry — H₀ is adopted wholesale from standard cosmological measurements (Cepheid distance ladder, Type Ia supernovae, CMB), all of which assume a spherical Earth in an expanding universe. None of the dome's geometric parameters (disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km) appear in the redshift formula. The 'aetheric redshift scale' is the Hubble radius wearing a different label.

additional_findings.no_dome_derivation: model.html lists λ_A = c/H₀ as a confirmed result, not a derived prediction. No equation connects disc_radius, firmament_height, or any dome parameter to H₀. The dome site attributes the framework to 'Tesla Principle 4' but provides no equations from Tesla's work that produce H₀.
additional_findings.low_z_test_trivial: The dome's four test objects (Virgo Cluster, Coma Cluster, Perseus Cluster, 3C273) are all at z < 0.15, where Hubble's Law is already known to be linear to high precision. Testing z = H₀d/c in the linear regime and finding agreement within 10% is not a prediction — it is confirming the definition. The interesting test would be at high-z where the dome's linear z = D/λ_A diverges from ΛCDM's nonlinear distance-redshift relation.
additional_findings.tired_light_equivalence: The dome's 'aetheric redshift' is phenomenologically identical to the tired-light hypothesis (Zwicky 1929), which proposed photon energy loss during propagation. Tired light was refuted by: (1) time dilation of Type Ia supernova light curves scaling as (1+z), observed by Goldhaber et al. (2001) — tired light predicts no time dilation; (2) preservation of surface brightness relations (Tolman test) — tired light predicts (1+z)^-1 dimming, expansion predicts (1+z)^-4, observations match expansion; (3) CMB blackbody spectrum — tired light cannot produce a perfect blackbody, as energy loss would distort the spectrum. The dome does not address any of these observational tests.
additional_findings.hubble_tension_hijacked: The dome invokes 'low-z deviations' and the Hubble tension (H₀ = 73 vs 67 km/s/Mpc) as evidence, but predicts neither value and offers no resolution. Both competing measurements use standard physics methods (Cepheid calibration vs CMB). The dome has no mechanism for producing one H₀ value over the other.

code_search.dome_model_html_finding: model.html contains: 'λ_A = 4,283 Mpc — aetheric redshift scale = c/H₀ (from Hubble constant)' in the unified theory section. No derivation. No connection to dome geometry.
code_search.dome_wins_html_finding: WIN-047 claims z = D/λ_A where λ_A = c/H₀ = 4,283 Mpc. Lists four verification objects (Virgo, Coma, Perseus, 3C273) at z < 0.15. Claims 'within 10%' agreement.
code_search.monitoring: No monitoring code exists for WIN-047 or any cosmological WIN (confirmed by curmudgeon reviews Cycle 1 + 2).
code_search.notes: λ_A = c/H₀ is a tautological relabeling: defining a scale using the standard cosmological Hubble constant and calling it 'aetheric.' No dome geometric parameter appears in the formula.

suggested_text: The dome's 'aetheric redshift scale' λ<sub>A</sub> = c/H₀ = 4,283 Mpc is identically the Hubble radius — a fundamental parameter of expanding-universe cosmology. The redshift formula z = D/λ<sub>A</sub> is algebraically Hubble's Law (z = H₀d/c). No dome parameter (disc radius, firmament height) appears in the formula; H₀ is adopted from standard cosmological measurements that assume a spherical Earth in an expanding universe. The dome has relabeled the Hubble radius as an 'aetheric wavelength' without changing the mathematics. Furthermore, this mechanism is phenomenologically identical to tired light (Zwicky 1929), which is refuted by supernova time dilation, Tolman surface brightness tests, and the CMB blackbody spectrum — none of which the dome addresses.
